_label: Encyclopédie, ou Dictionnaire raisonné des sciences, des arts et des métiers

identified_by.content: Encyclopédie, ou Dictionnaire raisonné des sciences, des arts et des métiers

referred_to_by.content: The complete first edition of the **Encyclopédie, ou Dictionnaire raisonné des sciences, des arts et des métiers**, edited by **Denis Diderot** (Langres, 1713 – Paris, 1784) and initially co-edited by **Jean le Rond d'Alembert** (Paris, 1717 – Paris, 1783), published in Paris and Amsterdam, 1751–1780. The complete first edition comprises **35 folio volumes**: 17 text volumes + 11 plates volumes + 4 supplement text volumes + 1 supplement plates volume + 2 analytical index volumes (*Table analytique*). Contains 71,818 articles and 3,129 plates, with more than 130 contributors. The work was condemned by the Parlement de Paris (1759) and placed on the Catholic Church's *Index Librorum Prohibitorum* by Pope Clement XIII (1759), forcing the completion of the text volumes in secret. Acquired by Teylers **in 1780** — four years before the museum's formal opening in 1784, making it one of the earliest documented acquisitions in the collection. Designated one of Teylers' **50 Topstukken** and central to the 2026 Teylers exhibition **"Gevaarlijke Boeken."**
